title: The Widow of Nain
subtitle: At the city gate, the Lord meets a mother carrying her only son to the grave

stillness: Be still, and put down for a while the world's noise.
The Lord journeys toward a city; let your soul go out to meet Him.
Wait in silence at the gate.
gospel: Jesus went on toward a city of Galilee called Nain,
and His disciples went with Him, and a great crowd.
And as He drew near the gate of the city,
behold, a dead man was being carried out,
the only son of his mother,
And when the Lord saw her, He had compassion on her,
and He said to her: Do not weep.
And He came near and touched the bier,
and those who carried it stood still.
And He said: Young man, I say to you, arise.
And the dead man sat up, and began to speak;
and He gave him back to his mother.
And fear seized them all, and they glorified God, saying:
A great prophet has risen among us, and God has visited His people.
behold: Attend to each thing as if you were present.
Behold the Lord drawing near to the gate of the city, where the crowd was thickest.
Behold the dead man carried out — the only son of a widowed mother.
She did not hope to bear children again; this one alone she had nursed.
He alone was her gladness; whatever was sweet and precious to her, he alone had been.
Behold a pitiable suffering, able to draw forth weeping and tears.
Behold the Lord, seeing her so sad and afflicted, moved with mercy and pity as a man.
Hear His sweet and consoling word: Do not weep.
As if He said: soon you will be consoled; cease to weep for one whom you will soon see rise alive.
Behold Him touch the bier, that the power of salvation might come by the touch of His body.
He who when living was not held by great palaces, when dying a small space suffices for his coffin.
When Alexander died, a philosopher said: he whom the whole world could not contain, today a little urn holds.
Behold those who carried him stand still, and dare go no further.
And He gave him back to his mother — that she who had been grieved in him might be consoled in him.
consider: This dead man is the human soul, dead through mortal sin.
His mother is the Church, who weeps over each one fallen into sin as a mother over an only son who has died.
The Church is called a widow, for now, as long as she wanders far from the Lord, she is deprived of her Bridegroom's embrace.
Consider the four who carry the dead: the four affections of our heart — joy and sorrow, hope and fear — when these bear the dead man by abuse.
Of whom Bernard says: they love what is unseemly, fear what they ought not, grieve in vain, and rejoice more vainly still.
Or the four bearers are love of sin, fear of penance, hope of amending, and presumption upon God's mercy.
The gate through which the dead man is carried out is one of the five senses, the betrayer of sin.
For he who looks upon a woman to lust after her carries out his death through the gate of the eyes.
He who opens his ear gladly to idle tales or filthy songs makes this gate of his soul a gate of death.
The coffin is the conscience of the sinner, in which, as on a bed, the evil conscience takes its rest.
There are three signs of spiritual death, as of bodily death.
The first: loss of action — for the lack of doing good is a sign of death.
The second: loss of feeling — when a man feels no spiritual blows nor warnings: They struck me, but I was not hurt; they dragged me, and I did not feel it.
The third: rigidity — when the heart is so hard it cannot be bent by compassion toward the neighbor, nor by obedience toward God.
Yet such a dead man, dead through mortal sin, is raised by God through the prayers of the Church.
For the heavier the fall, the more pleasing is the mercy of Him who lifts up, and the more certain the hope of salvation to the penitent.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, come into Nain, into my soul tossed by temptations;
draw near to the gate, holding back the senses, that they may not pass into the affections;
come by grace, touch the heart by correction;
let the bearers — the occasions and opportunities of sinning — stand still;
say to the soul lying in its sins that it may sit up by good will,
give it back to its mother, the nurse of grace, that it may stand fast in steadfastness;
and so may Your truth visit Your people — the powers of my soul, its affections and thoughts — by knowledge,
Your power by working, and Your goodness by keeping me. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Domine Jesu Christe, veni in Naim, animam meam fluctuantem tentationibus.
latinJewel.english: Lord Jesus Christ, come into Nain, into my soul tossed by temptations.
